definitions: A cognitive bias in which people with low ability at a task overestimate their own ability.
The phenomenon where incompetent individuals tend to overestimate their own competence, while highly competent individuals tend to underestimate their own competence.
examples: His overconfidence in his coding skills, despite numerous bugs, was a classic case of the Dunning-Kruger effect.
She suffered from the Dunning-Kruger effect, believing she was an expert after reading just one article.
The Dunning-Kruger effect explains why some people with limited knowledge are so vocal about complex topics.
